feature_name: Web Application Firewall
notes: WAF inline Prevention mode OR firewall_policy linked (WAF v2) → PASS; Detection mode → FAIL; no WAF → FAIL | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Network Security Group Support
notes: NSG supported on App Gateway v2 subnet with required rules (allow AzureLoadBalancer inbound, allow outbound internet). Private App Gateway deployment (GA May 2025) has enhanced NSG controls with subnet delegation to Microsoft.Network/applicationGateways. Conditional: specific inbound/outbound rules required; NSG flow logs not supported on v2 subnet. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/configuration-infrastructure

feature_name: Azure Private Link
notes: private_link_configurations non-empty → PASS (Private Link active on frontend) | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Disable Public Network Access
notes: No public_ip_address in any frontend_ip_configurations → PASS (private-only deployment) | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Monitor Anomalies and Threats Targeting Sensitive Data
notes: WAF Prevention mode → traffic inspection/anomaly detection active → PASS proxy | MCSB v3 baseline (Feb 2025): False/Not Applicable. No native DLP solution for App Gateway traffic. Use Azure Monitor + Defender for Cloud policies for anomaly detection on subnet. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline | WAF v2 provides anomaly scoring (CRS 3.2+) and DDoS attack detection. Integrate WAF logs with Azure Monitor for data anomaly tracking. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/web-application-firewall/ag/ag-overview

feature_name: Encrypt Data in Transit — Frontend SSL Policy
notes: ssl_policy.min_protocol_version in (TLSv1_2 / TLSv1_3) → PASS; None → FAIL (allows TLS 1.0/1.1) | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Encrypt Data in Transit — Backend TLS
notes: All backend_http_settings_collection[*].protocol=Https → PASS; any Http → FAIL

feature_name: Encrypt Data at Rest with Platform-Managed Keys
notes: AppGW configuration encrypted at rest with MS-managed keys by default — auto-PASS (static) | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Encrypt Data at Rest with Customer-Managed Key
notes: AppGW does not support CMK; MS-managed keys only → UNKNOWN with rationale | App Gateway not in Azure CMK support list as of May 2026. Managed keys encrypt configuration data at platform level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/encryption-customer-managed-keys-support

feature_name: Manage Cryptographic Keys using Key Management Service
notes: MCSB v3 baseline: DP-6 Key Management in Key Vault = False/Not Applicable. App Gateway does not support CMK or Key Vault key management. Note: DP-7 (certificate management via KV) IS supported — separate feature. Phase 48 cache incorrectly marked as now_applicable_native — reverted. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Certificate Management using Azure Key Vault
notes: App Gateway v2 supports TLS certificate management via Azure Key Vault. Certificates stored in Key Vault are auto-rotated every 4 hours. Requires user-assigned managed identity with Key Vault Secrets User role. MCSB v3 baseline: True/False/Customer. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/key-vault-certs

feature_name: Use Centralized Identity and Authentication System — Disable Local Auth
notes: AppGW has no SAS/shared-key; ARM RBAC only → UNKNOWN with rationale | MCSB v3 baseline: False/Not Applicable for local auth disable. App Gateway has no concept of local auth methods at data plane. Management access via Azure AD RBAC only. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Use Centralized Identity and Authentication System — AAD Auth Required
notes: JWT validation via Microsoft Entra ID now in PUBLIC PREVIEW (Jan 2026). App Gateway validates JWTs on HTTPS listeners, blocks invalid tokens (401), injects x-msft-entra-identity header to backend. Conditional: requires Standard_v2/WAF_v2 SKU, HTTPS listener, ARM API 2025-03-01+; PREVIEW status (not for production yet). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/json-web-token-overview

feature_name: Use Azure AD Managed Identities for Azure Resource Authentication
notes: App Gateway uses user-assigned managed identity to authenticate to Azure Key Vault for TLS certificate retrieval. Managed identity is optional at deploy time but required for Key Vault cert integration. MCSB note: 'managed identity can be used by Application Gateway to authenticate to Azure Key Vault'. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/key-vault-certs

feature_name: Use Azure AD Service Principals for Azure Resource Authentication
notes: SP assignments via ARM RBAC; not readable per-gateway without azure-mgmt-authorization → UNKNOWN | MCSB v3 baseline: feature_supported=False for per-gateway SP. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Restrict Resource Access Based on Conditions — Conditional Access
notes: CA policies Entra tenant-level; not ARM-readable → UNKNOWN | MCSB v3 baseline: False/Not Applicable. App Gateway itself does not enforce Conditional Access policies at the data plane. CA applies to management plane (Azure Portal/ARM) access only. JWT preview (IM-1) provides token validation but is not Conditional Access. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Restrict the Exposure of Credential and Secrets — Use Azure Key Vault
notes: App Gateway v2 stores TLS certificates in managed Azure Key Vault automatically when uploaded. Key Vault integration supported via both access policy and RBAC models. MCSB v3 baseline: True/False/Customer. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Enable Threat Detection Capabilities — Microsoft Defender for Application Gateway
notes: No Defender for AppGW product; WAF (NS-1) covers L7 threat detection → UNKNOWN | MCSB v3 baseline: LT-1 Defender for Service = False/Not Applicable. No dedicated Defender plan for App Gateway. WAF v1 integration with Defender for Cloud security alerts was retired Sep 2024. App Gateway logs can feed into Sentinel/Azure Monitor for threat detection. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Enable Logging for Azure Resources
notes: MonitorManagementClient.diagnostic_settings.list(gw.id); any enabled log → PASS; key: AccessLog + FirewallLog | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Ensure Regular Automated Backups — Azure Backup Service
notes: Azure Backup does not support AppGW config; ARM template export is manual only → UNKNOWN | Network infrastructure resource — configuration is declarative (ARM templates, Azure Policy). Azure Backup is a data backup service for compute/storage. Backup/Recovery for this resource type means ARM export or Resource Graph snapshots, not Azure Backup integration. Feature not supported in MCSB v3 baseline; no change as of June 2026. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/backup-overview

feature_name: Ensure Regular Automated Backups — Native Soft-Delete and Purge Protection
notes: No soft-delete or purge-protection for AppGW config; recovery via IaC templates → UNKNOWN | Network infrastructure resource — configuration is declarative (ARM templates, Azure Policy). Azure Backup is a data backup service for compute/storage. Backup/Recovery for this resource type means ARM export or Resource Graph snapshots, not Azure Backup integration. Feature not supported in MCSB v3 baseline; no change as of June 2026. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/backup/backup-overview

feature_name: Use Only Approved Azure Services — Azure Policy Support
notes: Tags presence as proxy for policy governance; empty tags → FAIL | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Use Only Approved Applications in Virtual Machine — Defender for Cloud AAC
notes: PaaS — Adaptive Application Controls target VMs and Arc servers only | Network infrastructure resource with no customer-managed compute. Microsoft Defender for Servers/Endpoints is not applicable — no VMs are exposed to customers. Anti-malware is managed at the Azure infrastructure layer. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/infrastructure

feature_name: Separate and Limit Highly Privileged/Administrative Users — Disable Local Admin
notes: AppGW has no local admin/shared-key concept; ARM RBAC only → UNKNOWN with rationale | MCSB v3 baseline: False/Not Applicable. App Gateway has no local administrative account concept. All management access via Azure AD RBAC at resource/resource-group level. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Follow Just Enough Administration Principle — RBAC for Data Plane
notes: AppGW has no separate data-plane RBAC; JEA enforced at ARM RBAC level → UNKNOWN with rationale | MCSB v3 baseline: False/Not Applicable for data plane RBAC. App Gateway has no customer-accessible data plane requiring fine-grained RBAC. Management plane access controlled via Azure RBAC (Contributor, Reader roles). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Determine Access Process for Microsoft Support — Customer Lockbox
notes: MCSB v3 baseline: Customer Lockbox = False/Not Applicable. App Gateway not in Customer Lockbox supported services list. Phase 48 cache incorrectly marked as now_applicable_native — reverted. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/customer-lockbox-overview

feature_name: EDR Solution
notes: PaaS — no customer compute; EDR not applicable | Phase48-cache: PaaS — no customer-accessible compute; EDR agents cannot be deployed [NEEDS-EXA-URL]

feature_name: Anti-Malware Solution
notes: PaaS — no customer OS layer | Phase48-cache: PaaS — no customer OS layer [NEEDS-EXA-URL]

feature_name: Anti-Malware Solution Health Monitoring
notes: PaaS — ES-2 not applicable therefore ES-3 not applicable | Phase48-cache: PaaS — ES-2 not applicable therefore ES-3 not applicable [NEEDS-EXA-URL]

feature_name: Azure Automation State Configuration
notes: PaaS — DSC targets VMs and Arc machines | Phase48-cache: PaaS — Automation DSC targets VMs and Arc machines [NEEDS-EXA-URL] | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed still_not_applicable: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Azure Policy Guest Configuration Agent
notes: PaaS — Guest Config requires customer guest OS | Phase48-cache: PaaS — Guest Config requires customer-accessible guest OS [NEEDS-EXA-URL] | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed still_not_applicable: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Custom Container Images
notes: PaaS — Microsoft manages AppGW runtime | Phase48-cache: PaaS — Microsoft manages Application Gateway runtime [NEEDS-EXA-URL] | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed still_not_applicable: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Custom VM Images
notes: PaaS — no customer VM images | Phase48-cache: PaaS — no customer-supplied VM images [NEEDS-EXA-URL] | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed still_not_applicable: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Vulnerability Assessment using Microsoft Defender
notes: PaaS — no stack to scan | Phase48-cache: PaaS — no software stack to scan on customer side [NEEDS-EXA-URL] | MCSB v3 baseline confirmed still_not_applicable: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/baselines/application-gateway-security-baseline

feature_name: Azure Automation Update Management
notes: PaaS — Microsoft patches AppGW infrastructure | Phase48-cache: PaaS — Microsoft patches AppGW infrastructure [NEEDS-EXA-URL]

feature_name: Private Application Gateway — No Public IP Deployment
notes: v2 gap: Application Gateway v2 now supports private-only frontend IP configuration (no public IP required) — closes NS-7 public network access disable gap. Requires: v2 SKU (Standard_v2 or WAF_v2), subnet delegation to Microsoft.Network/applicationGateways (mandatory from May 5 2025), private-only frontend IP config (no PublicIPAddress property). Conditional: opt-in at deployment time; existing AppGW with public IP requires rebuild. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/application-gateway/application-gateway-private-deployment | Q2 NS-7 Exa search June 2026 confirmed GA.
